data.name: Dot-Grid Board System
data.summary: Implements a dot-grid board where dots define snake positions and bend paths. Board sizes range from a minimum of 3x3 to a maximum of 40x40 dots. Supports pinch-to-zoom gesture for larger boards using two fingers.
key_behaviors: Defines snake positions by dots on a grid
Allows bends in snake paths at dots
Supports pinch-to-zoom gesture on large boards

data.name: Snake Representation and Directional Behavior
data.summary: Snakes occupy sequences of dots with a facing direction (Left, Right, Up, Down). Direction determines the exit trajectory when tapped. Snake rendering includes curved bends along dot paths.

data.name: Tap-to-Move Mechanic for Snakes
data.summary: Players tap snakes to slide them off the board if the path to board edge is unobstructed. Successful moves trigger an exit animation and remove the snake, crediting the player. Wrong moves (path blocked) cause a one-time health penalty per snake.
key_behaviors: Deduct health on first wrong tap per snake
No repeated health penalties on same snake

data.name: Health and Lives System
data.summary: Each level has configurable health representing allowable wrong moves (default 3). When health reaches zero, the level fails, triggering Revive or Failed screens.
key_behaviors: Show failure and revive options on zero health

data.name: Level Completion and Failure Flow
data.summary: Winning the level requires all snakes to exit, awarding coins and showing Result screen. Failing occurs when health reaches zero, showing Revive/Failed popups with options to revive (pay or watch ad) or restart (reset progress).
key_behaviors: Trigger win screen and coin award
Trigger fail screen if health zero

data.name: Wall Obstacle with Countdown and Collision Feedback
data.summary: Walls block snake movement. Each snake exit reduces the Wall's countdown by one. When countdown hits zero, the Wall disappears. Collision with Wall costs health and triggers collision animation.
key_behaviors: Decrement Wall countdown per snake escape
On collision, reduce health and show animation

data.name: Black Hole Portal Element
data.summary: Black Holes act as paired portals: snakes entering one exit from its pair or directly off board. Teleport includes swirl/shrink enter and expand exit animations.
key_behaviors: Teleport snake between paired Black Holes or off board
Play swirl/shrink entry and expand exit animations

data.name: Gameplay Element Extensibility and First Encounter Popups
data.summary: System designed to support future elements like moving barriers and ice tiles. First encounter with any element triggers a popup with icon, description, and an OK button with pulse animation.
key_behaviors: Show new element popup on first encounter
Include icon, description, and animated OK button

data.name: Hint Booster System
data.summary: A booster that highlights one snake that can currently escape. Costs 300 Coin or watching one ad per use, and is inventory-based.

data.name: Grid Vision (Ruler) Booster
data.summary: Displays directional exit arrows on all snakes simultaneously. Costs 900 Coin or watching one ad per use, inventory-based.

data.name: Booster Acquisition and Use UX Flow
data.summary: Booster acquisition via IAP, daily missions, daily spin, tournament rewards, and no-ads bundles. First introduction popup shows booster info, followed by tutorial finger pointing to booster button, and usage reduces inventory by one.
key_behaviors: Display new booster popup with icon and description
Trigger tutorial finger animation for booster use
Deduct from booster inventory on activation

data.name: Progressive In-Game Tutorial System
data.summary: Triggers tutorials on level milestones or first encounters, fading out after the player completes prompted actions. Includes animated finger with specified animation states and durations.

data.name: Level Structure and Difficulty Progression
data.summary: Levels numbered sequentially without chapter locks. Difficulty grows by increasing board size, snake count, obstacles, and reducing health margins. Levels grouped into themed chapters for visual variety.

data.name: Level Unlocking and Tournament Gate
data.summary: Levels unlock sequentially after completion. Tournament feature unlocks at Level 14, gating competitive play.
key_behaviors: Tournament unlock trigger at specific level

data.name: Coin Economy Sources and Sinks
data.summary: Coins earned from level completions, ads, daily activities, missions, spins, and IAP packs. Coins spent on boosters and revives, with detailed pricing and bundles including no-ads options.
key_behaviors: Coin expenditure on boosters and revive
IAP coin packs and bundles offered
Ad types managed with frequency caps and disabling on no-ads purchase

data.name: Daily Systems: Missions, Spin, Challenges, Rewards, and Video Bar
data.summary: Includes daily missions with tiered rewards, one free spin with ad-based extras, a unique daily challenge puzzle, daily login rewards, and progressive ad watching bar for increasing rewards.
key_behaviors: Daily spin with animation and rewards
Daily unique challenge level and replay

data.name: Tournament System with Streak Multiplier and Leaderboard
data.summary: Players join starting Level 14, scoring with win streak multiplier increasing per win, managed server-side with leaderboards and reward tiers based on ranks.
key_behaviors: Win streak multiplier and reset conditions

data.name: Play With Friend Asynchronous/Real-Time Mode
data.summary: Enables match where two players complete the same level asynchronously or real-time. Features scoring with base and time bonuses, match countdown timer, result screen with score and snake count comparison, and friend management flow.

data.name: Screen Inventory and Navigation Flow
data.summary: Defines UI screens including splash, home/dashboard, gameplay HUD, results, shop, daily features, tournament, friend system, and settings. Navigation flows from splash to home and branches to various features like shop and gameplay.
key_behaviors: Navigation paths from splash through main features

data.name: Localization, Themes, and Layout Support
data.summary: Multi-language support including Portuguese, with design for text expansion. Includes dark mode full visual variant and landscape orientation layout.

data.name: Animation Specifications for Gameplay and UI
data.summary: Includes snake movement, exit, wrong move feedback, tutorial finger states, popups, boosters, walls, black hole teleport, spin wheel, and win streak visuals with detailed timing and effects.
key_behaviors: Spin wheel and win streak particle effects

data.name: Player Data Cloud Save and Sync
data.summary: Persist player data including level progress, coins, boosters, settings, mission and tournament progress. Syncs at session start with local caching to maintain 60fps gameplay.
key_behaviors: Cloud save and sync of player data

data.name: Daily Reset and Remote Configuration Services
data.summary: Server-side UTC resets for daily systems like missions and spins. Provides remote config for economy tuning, tournament schedules, rewards, and feature flags for seasonal events.
key_behaviors: Remote config for economy and events

data.name: Daily Challenge Backend Service
data.summary: Backend serves a unique daily puzzle level JSON to all players with support for replaying past challenges on request.
key_behaviors: Support replay of past daily challenges

data.name: Analytics Event Tracking
data.summary: Tracks key gameplay and app events including level starts/completions/fails, booster usage, revives, IAP purchases, ad watches, and session starts.
key_behaviors: Track and log critical player events

data.name: Level Data Format and Editor with Validation
data.summary: Serialized level files include board size, dots, snake positions/directions, obstacles, and health. Unity Editor tool enables level design and an automated solvability checker validates at least one solution before shipping.
